title: GitHub ruleset/branch protection for main is plan-gated (private repo on Free plan)
affects: owner directive 2026-07-17: protect main (require PRs, required status checks, block force pushes and deletion, require conversation resolution, no bypass)
server-side enforcement of the PR-based merge workflow
detail: Owner directive 2026-07-17 requires a GitHub ruleset on main. Inspection (2026-07-17): repo martin10101/nyc-buildability is private on the GitHub Free plan; GET /repos/.../rulesets and GET /repos/.../branches/main/protection both return HTTP 403 'Upgrade to GitHub Pro or make this repository public to enable this feature'. Main currently has NO server-side protection and none can be created until the plan constraint clears. INTERIM ENFORCEMENT (orchestrator-side, active now): (1) .claude/settings.local.json denies direct pushes to main and force pushes; (2) all implementation and material documentation work flows task branch -> push branch -> PR -> CI green -> merge -> verify remote main -> delete branch/worktree; (3) routine merges proceed autonomously after independent gates + GitHub checks pass, per the directive.
exact_human_action: Owner chooses one: (a) upgrade the GitHub account/repo to GitHub Pro (paid - owner-only), or (b) make the repository public (owner decision; consider that this is a legally sensitive project). After either, the orchestrator will create the ruleset: require pull requests (0 required approvals so routine tasks merge autonomously), required status checks (CI + secret-scan jobs), block force pushes, block branch deletion, require conversation resolution, no bypass actors.
how_it_will_be_tested: gh api repos/martin10101/nyc-buildability/rulesets returns 200 and lists the active ruleset; a direct push to main is rejected by GitHub; a PR with failing checks cannot merge; a PR with passing checks merges without owner approval.

audit_log.note: RESOLVED: owner chose option (b) - repository made public 2026-07-20. Orchestrator created ruleset protect-main (id 19191154, enforcement active) per the recorded spec: branch target ~DEFAULT_BRANCH; rules deletion-block, non_fast_forward (force-push block), pull_request (0 required approvals, required_review_thread_resolution true, merge method merge), required_status_checks with the 8 CI job contexts (credential scan, api, contracts, contracts-schema-bundle, contracts-typegen, control-plane, web, web-e2e); bypass_actors empty. Verified: POST returned 200 with active enforcement. Orchestrator-side interim enforcement rules in settings.local.json stay in place as defense in depth.
